question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: A confirmed phishing/credential‑harvest incident where investigators found convincing fake Microsoft login pages and recommended emergency containment (geo/firewall controls) and account exceptions. The ticket records the phishing artifacts, the emergency mitigation proposal, and the need to carve out exceptions for critical accounts [This exact workflow is recorded in the phishing incident ticket]

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: A case where an integration stopped authenticating in background while the same account still worked interactively in a browser: investigators found ADFS / client‑registration parameters were misconfigured (EnableJWT / RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders) and that change broke non‑browser/native automation while leaving interactive browser logins functional

26.11.24 13:36 Portal note added

27.11.24 13:29 Ticket assigned | TI-1385257
The ticket TI-1385257 has been assigned to you.
Company: Customer company 11AG
Contact: Employee 14

27.11.24 13:29 Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1385257
Hello Employee 14,
The ticket TI-1385257 is no longer in your responsibility.
It was transferred from Admin to Employee 24.

28.11.24 09:01 Ticket status changed to In Progress
The ticket TI-1385257 was set to status In Progress.

09.12.24 15:58 Re: Ticket created: TI-1385257
Hi,
we cannot connect to the CE TRAIN server again. Could you please reopen this ticket?
Thank you and best regards
Employee 14

18.12.24 00:33 Ticket closed | TI-1385257
Dear Employee 14,
Your ticket from 26.11.24 10:07 has been closed.
Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Notes:
26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16.
26.11.24 Ticket assigned

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: A tenant case where OWA (browser) continued to work but non‑browser SMTP/legacy clients failed because Conditional Access / SMTP AUTH policy interactions required scoped exceptions to restore service — this was handled by identity/mail teams and required policy adjustments rather than only helpdesk steps

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox / sporadic SMTP error
Ticket number: TI-1548823
Company: Customer Company 22 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 08
Owner: Employee 34
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Closure
Closure date: 22.01.25 16:42
Closure text: Root cause identified (Hybrid Connector configuration). SMTP Auth adjusted, test successful. Ticket can be closed.
Activities
Emails:
Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22
From: Contact Person 08
To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company
Hello everyone
Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message:
0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed
SMTP server not responding
Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected.
Internet connectivity is generally working.
Could you please check this urgently?
Kind regards
Contact Person 08
14.01.25 08:21
Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox
Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08
Priority: High
14.01.25 08:29
Ticket assigned: Employee 34
Status changes
14.01.25 08:35
Status: In Progress
Notes:
14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34
Contact established with Contact Person 08.
Additional information:
• Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11)
• Webmail (OWA) works
• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending
• Receiving works
Initial assessment:
→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting
Actions performed:
• Recreated Outlook profile
• Removed and re-added account
• Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS)
• Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning
• Checked client firewall
Result: No improvement
14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No
Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay
14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation
Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist).
Reason:
Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy
14.01.25 11:15
Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823
Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering
14.01.25 11:16
Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07
Further analysis (System Engineer)
15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs
21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback
All users can send normally again.
No further issues observed.
Ticket closure
22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK
Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication
Ticket number: TI-722423
Company: Customer Company 11
Contact person: Contact Person 8
Owner: Employee 11
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 27.03.24 12:04
Closure
Closure date: 14.06.24 16:10
Closure text: Incident successfully resolved
Activities
Emails:
27.03.24 13:10 | TI-722423 | KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication | Employee 11 | Contact Person 8
Hello Contact Person 8, hello Contact Person 2,
Together with the network team, I adjusted the firewall rules and then reinstalled the connector – this time without proxy configuration. After the reinstallation, the connector was displayed as “green” again in Azure (this step is required so that it can properly communicate with the Intune service).
Since we had identified login issues, we temporarily excluded the service account from the MFA requirement.
After completing the installation, I removed the “Intune Admin” role from the service account again. If we need to reinstall the connector, this role must be temporarily reassigned.
During the installation, I followed the Microsoft guide
“Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.”
Attached you will find logs to quickly verify whether the connector is operating correctly. See blueprint_1.
Please do not make any changes to the settings without prior coordination.
If issues occur during the Intune deployment, you may contact the Helpdesk or the WPS team at any time.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Multiple independent subsystems failed simultaneously after a suspected phishing event: (a) credential compromise risk and emergency CA/geolocation changes that now block legitimate traveling users, (b) automated/service accounts failing, and (c) one integration where interactive browser auth still works but background/auth automation fails. The indexed incidents above show that this pattern (phishing → emergency containment changes → legitimate user/service disruption → non‑browser/integration auth failures) required coordinated engineer involvement (identity/AD/ADFS, Conditional Access, network/firewall, and application owners) to diagnose and safely remediate
context:

Together with the network team, I adjusted the firewall rules and then reinstalled the connector – this time without proxy configuration. After the reinstallation, the connector was displayed as “green” again in Azure (this step is required so that it can properly communicate with the Intune service).
Since we had identified login issues, we temporarily excluded the service account from the MFA requirement.
After completing the installation, I removed the “Intune Admin” role from the service account again. If we need to reinstall the connector, this role must be temporarily reassigned.
During the installation, I followed the Microsoft guide
“Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.”
Attached you will find logs to quickly verify whether the connector is operating correctly. See blueprint_1.
Please do not make any changes to the settings without prior coordination.
If issues occur during the Intune deployment, you may contact the Helpdesk or the WPS team at any time.

Dear Employee 14,

Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Notes:
26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16.
26.11.24 Ticket assigned

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox / sporadic SMTP error
Ticket number: TI-1548823
Company: Customer Company 22 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 08
Owner: Employee 34
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Closure
Closure date: 22.01.25 16:42
Closure text: Root cause identified (Hybrid Connector configuration). SMTP Auth adjusted, test successful. Ticket can be closed.
Activities
Emails:
Created on: 14.01.25 08:17
Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22
From: Contact Person 08
To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company
Hello everyone
Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message:
0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed
SMTP server not responding
Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected.
Internet connectivity is generally working.
Could you please check this urgently?
Kind regards
Contact Person 08
14.01.25 08:21
Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox
Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08
Priority: High
14.01.25 08:29
Ticket assigned: Employee 34
Status changes
14.01.25 08:35
Status: In Progress
Notes:
14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34
Contact established with Contact Person 08.
Additional information:
• Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11)
• Webmail (OWA) works
• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending
• Receiving works
Initial assessment:
→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting
Actions performed:
• Recreated Outlook profile
• Removed and re-added account
• Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS)
• Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning
• Checked client firewall
Result: No improvement
14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No
Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay
14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation
Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist).
Reason:
Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy
14.01.25 11:15
Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823
Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering
14.01.25 11:16
Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07
Further analysis (System Engineer)
15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs
21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback
All users can send normally again.
No further issues observed.
Ticket closure
22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK
Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk

27.03.24 13:10 | TI-722423 | KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication | Employee 11 | Contact Person 8
Hello Contact Person 8, hello Contact Person 2,
Together with the network team, I adjusted the firewall rules and then reinstalled the connector – this time without proxy configuration. After the reinstallation, the connector was displayed as “green” again in Azure (this step is required so that it can properly communicate with the Intune service).
Since we had identified login issues, we temporarily excluded the service account from the MFA requirement.
After completing the installation, I removed the “Intune Admin” role from the service account again. If we need to reinstall the connector, this role must be temporarily reassigned.
During the installation, I followed the Microsoft guide
“Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.”
Attached you will find logs to quickly verify whether the connector is operating correctly. See blueprint_1.
Please do not make any changes to the settings without prior coordination.
If issues occur during the Intune deployment, you may contact the Helpdesk or the WPS team at any time.
